Exercise Sheet 3

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import scipy.stats as stats
import pandas as pd

2

a

If \(W_t\sim Uniform(1,6)\) (discrete), then with \(\mu = 7/2\) and \(\sigma = \sqrt{35/12}\), \(Z_t = \frac{W_t-\mu}{\sigma}\)

More generally, if \(X\sim Uniform(a,b)\) (discrete)

\[\mu = E[X] = \frac{a+b}{2}, \sigma^2 = \frac{(b-a+1)^2-1}{12}\] 
w = np.random.randint(1,7,1_000_000)
z = (w-7/2)*(6/np.sqrt(105))
print(np.mean(z),np.var(z))
-0.0016799143855736052 0.9993530636019428
b
def GARCH_1_1(alpha0,alpha1,beta,T,SWN_func,dist_params):


    if SWN_func.__name__ == "normal":
        w = SWN_func(dist_params[0],dist_params[1],T)

        z = (w-dist_params[0])/np.sqrt(dist_params[1])

    if SWN_func.__name__ == "randint":
        w = SWN_func(dist_params[0],dist_params[1]+1,T)

        a = dist_params[0]
        b = dist_params[1]
        mu = (a+b)/2
        var = ((b-a+1)**2-1)/12
        z = (w-mu)/np.sqrt(var)

    x_var = alpha0/(1-(alpha1+beta))
    X = np.zeros(shape=(T))
    sigma = np.zeros(shape=(T))
    sigma[0] = np.sqrt(x_var) 
    X[0] = sigma[0]*z[0]
    for t in range(1,T):
        sigma[t] = np.sqrt(alpha0+alpha1*X[t-1]**2+beta*sigma[t-1]**2)
        X[t] = sigma[t]*z[t]

    return X,z,sigma
alpha0 = 100; alpha1 = 0.5; beta = 0.4; T=2000
X,Z,Sigma = GARCH_1_1(alpha0,alpha1,beta,T=T,SWN_func=np.random.randint,dist_params=[1,6])
c
plt.figure(figsize=(18,12))
plt.subplot(3,2,1)
plt.plot(X)
plt.title(fr"$X_t$")
plt.subplot(3,2,2)
plt.plot(Sigma)
plt.title(fr"$\sigma_t$")
plt.subplot(3,2,3)
plt.hist(X)
plt.title(fr"Histogram -- $X_t$")
plt.subplot(3,2,4)
plt.hist(Z)
plt.title(fr"Histogram -- $Z_t$")
plt.subplot(3,2,5)
stats.probplot(X, dist="norm",plot=plt)
plt.title(fr"$X_t$")

plt.subplot(3,2,6)
stats.probplot(Z, dist="norm",plot=plt)
plt.title(fr"$Z_t$")

plt.show()

5

GDAXI = pd.read_csv("GDAXI.csv")
GDAXI = GDAXI.iloc[::-1].reset_index(drop=True)
GSPC = pd.read_csv("GSPC.csv")
GSPC = GSPC.iloc[::-1].reset_index(drop=True)

GSPC
Date Open High Low Close
0 01/03/00 1455.22 1478.00 1438.36 1455.22
1 01/04/00 1399.42 1455.22 1397.43 1399.42
2 01/05/00 1402.11 1413.27 1377.68 1402.11
3 01/06/00 1403.45 1411.90 1392.02 1403.45
4 01/07/00 1441.47 1441.47 1400.53 1441.47
... ... ... ... ... ...
5279 12/24/20 3694.03 3703.82 3689.32 3703.06
5280 12/28/20 3723.03 3740.51 3723.03 3735.36
5281 12/29/20 3750.01 3756.12 3723.31 3727.04
5282 12/30/20 3736.19 3744.63 3730.21 3732.04
5283 12/31/20 3733.27 3760.20 3726.88 3756.07

5284 rows × 5 columns

print(GDAXI.keys())
Index(['Date', ' Open', ' High', ' Low', ' Close'], dtype='str')
absol_return = lambda series : series.diff()
log_return = lambda series : np.log(series/series.shift(1))
add_yr = lambda series : ("20"+series.str[-2:]).astype(int)
ticks_loc = lambda df : [df[df["Year"] == y].index[0] for y in np.unique(df["Year"])]


GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"],GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"] = absol_return(GDAXI[" Close"]),log_return(GDAXI[" Close"])
GSPC["Absolute Return (Closing)"],GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"] = absol_return(GSPC[" Close"]),log_return(GSPC[" Close"])
GDAXI["Year"] = add_yr(GDAXI["Date"])
GSPC["Year"] = add_yr(GSPC["Date"])

GDAXI_ticks= ticks_loc(GDAXI)
GSPC_ticks= ticks_loc(GSPC)

GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"]-GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"]
plt.figure(figsize=(12,12))

plt.subplot(3,2,1)
GDAXI_col = GDAXI[" Close"]
GDAXI_stats = f"Max: {GDAXI_col.max():.2f}\nMin: {GDAXI_col.min():.2f}\nStart: {GDAXI_col.iloc[0]:.2f}\nEnd: {GDAXI_col.iloc[-1]:.2f}"

plt.text(0.02, 0.9, GDAXI_stats,
         transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
         verticalalignment='top',
         fontsize=7)
plt.plot(GDAXI["Date"],GDAXI[" Close"])
plt.xticks(GDAXI_ticks,rotation=90)
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Prices")
plt.title("German Dax (Closing Prices 2000-2020)")

plt.subplot(3,2,2)
GSPC_col = GSPC[" Close"]
GSPC_stats = f"Max: {GSPC_col.max():.2f}\nMin: {GSPC_col.min():.2f}\nStart: {GSPC_col.iloc[0]:.2f}\nEnd: {GSPC_col.iloc[-1]:.2f}"

plt.text(0.02, 0.9, GSPC_stats,
         transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
         verticalalignment='top',
         fontsize=7)
plt.plot(GSPC["Date"],GSPC[" Close"])
plt.xticks(GSPC_ticks,rotation=90)
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.title("US S&P 500  (Closing Prices 2000-2020)")

plt.subplot(3,2,3)
plt.plot(GDAXI["Date"],GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"])
plt.title("German Dax (Absolute Closing Returns 2000-2020)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.xticks(GDAXI_ticks,rotation=90)
plt.ylabel(fr"Absolute Closing Return $R_t$")
plt.subplot(3,2,4)
plt.plot(GSPC["Date"],GSPC["Absolute Return (Closing)"])
plt.title("US S&P 500 (Absolute Closing Returns 2000-2020)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.xticks(GSPC_ticks,rotation=90)


plt.subplot(3,2,5)
plt.plot(GDAXI["Date"],GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"])
plt.title("German Dax (Log Closing Returns 2000-2020)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.xticks(GDAXI_ticks,rotation=90)

plt.ylabel(fr"log return (Closing)")

plt.subplot(3,2,6)
plt.plot(GSPC["Date"],GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"])
plt.title("US S&P 500(Log Closing Returns 2000-2020)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.xticks(GSPC_ticks,rotation=90)


plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Comments * Both series look very similar, the S&P being a bit smoother in the sense that it is a little bit less volatile (but not significantly less). This makes sense, both represent a pool of large companies, the mean difference is the market size and number of company, explaining the slightly greater volatility of the German Dax * There are volatility clusters of both series happen at the same time, namely dot-com bubble in the early 2000's, the great financial crisis late 2008 to early 2009, and ultimately COVID-19 (early 2020) * Lastly, there is one thing to pinpoint, if one would have invested in the german index the investment would have roughly doubled in nomial terms whereas for the S&P 500 the nomial return is slighty higher. One could then argue that you pay for more risk not the other way around which is ofcourse not ideal.

Stationarity

First, it is rather clear that the raw closing prices (in both cases) are very far from being stationary, there is a strong violation of the stationary hypothesis, namely there is a strong trend.

Second, the absolute returns also violate stationarity, there are clusters (which one its own does not violate stationarity), however there is an increase in the volatility over time (for these are on a relative scale)

Third, the log returns seem to be stationary there is no clear argument for a violation, one could even argue that the series weak white noise (mean is zero, over the long term variance is constant, and there is no clear evidence of serial dependence per say)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))
plt.tight_layout()
max_lag = round(10*np.log10(len(GDAXI)))
lags = np.arange(1, max_lag)
acf_GDAXI = [GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].autocorr(lag=i) for i in lags]
acf_GSPC = [GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].autocorr(lag=i) for i in lags]

n_GDAXI = len(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
n_GSPC = len(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
conf_GDAXI = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GDAXI)
conf_GSPC = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GSPC)

mins_GDAXI = min(min(acf_GDAXI), -conf_GDAXI)
maxs_GDAXI = max(max(acf_GDAXI), conf_GDAXI)
mins_GSPC = min(min(acf_GSPC), -conf_GSPC)
maxs_GSPC = max(max(acf_GSPC), conf_GSPC)
abs_max_GDAXI = max(abs(mins_GDAXI), maxs_GDAXI, conf_GDAXI)
abs_max_GSPC = max(abs(mins_GSPC), maxs_GSPC, conf_GSPC)

plt.subplot(2,2,1)
plt.stem(lags, acf_GDAXI)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GDAXI*1.1, maxs_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Autocorrelation (Log return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Autocorrelation")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,2)
plt.stem(lags, acf_GSPC)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GSPC*1.1, maxs_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Autocorrelation (Log return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Autocorrelation")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,3)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(acf_GDAXI))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs Autocorrelation (Log return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs Autocorrelation")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,4)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(acf_GSPC))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs Autocorrelation (Log return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs Autocorrelation")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.show()

from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import pacf

plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))
plt.tight_layout()
max_lag = round(10*np.log10(len(GDAXI)))
lags = np.arange(1, max_lag)

pacf_GDAXI = pacf(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), nlags=max_lag-1)[1:]
pacf_GSPC = pacf(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), nlags=max_lag-1)[1:]

n_GDAXI = len(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
n_GSPC = len(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
conf_GDAXI = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GDAXI)
conf_GSPC = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GSPC)

mins_GDAXI = min(min(pacf_GDAXI), -conf_GDAXI)
maxs_GDAXI = max(max(pacf_GDAXI), conf_GDAXI)
mins_GSPC = min(min(pacf_GSPC), -conf_GSPC)
maxs_GSPC = max(max(pacf_GSPC), conf_GSPC)
abs_max_GDAXI = max(abs(mins_GDAXI), maxs_GDAXI, conf_GDAXI)
abs_max_GSPC = max(abs(mins_GSPC), maxs_GSPC, conf_GSPC)

plt.subplot(2,2,1)
plt.stem(lags, pacf_GDAXI)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GDAXI*1.1, maxs_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("PACF (Log return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,2)
plt.stem(lags, pacf_GSPC)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GSPC*1.1, maxs_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("PACF (Log return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,3)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(pacf_GDAXI))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs PACF (Log return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,4)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(pacf_GSPC))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs PACF (Log return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.show()
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import pacf

plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))
plt.tight_layout()
max_lag = round(10*np.log10(len(GDAXI)))
lags = np.arange(1, max_lag)

pacf_GDAXI = pacf(GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna(), nlags=max_lag-1)[1:]
pacf_GSPC = pacf(GSPC["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna(), nlags=max_lag-1)[1:]

n_GDAXI = len(GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna())
n_GSPC = len(GSPC["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna())
conf_GDAXI = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GDAXI)
conf_GSPC = 1.96 / np.sqrt(n_GSPC)

mins_GDAXI = min(min(pacf_GDAXI), -conf_GDAXI)
maxs_GDAXI = max(max(pacf_GDAXI), conf_GDAXI)
mins_GSPC = min(min(pacf_GSPC), -conf_GSPC)
maxs_GSPC = max(max(pacf_GSPC), conf_GSPC)
abs_max_GDAXI = max(abs(mins_GDAXI), maxs_GDAXI, conf_GDAXI)
abs_max_GSPC = max(abs(mins_GSPC), maxs_GSPC, conf_GSPC)

plt.subplot(2,2,1)
plt.stem(lags, pacf_GDAXI)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GDAXI*1.1, maxs_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("PACF (Simple return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,2)
plt.stem(lags, pacf_GSPC)
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.axhline(y=-conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(mins_GSPC*1.1, maxs_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("PACF (Simple return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,3)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(pacf_GDAXI))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GDAXI*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs PACF (Simple return German DAX 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.subplot(2,2,4)
plt.stem(lags, np.abs(pacf_GSPC))
plt.axhline(y=conf_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='blue', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlim(0.5, max_lag - 0.5)
plt.ylim(0, abs_max_GSPC*1.1)
plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("Abs PACF (Simple return US S&P500 2000-2020)")
plt.ylabel("Abs PACF")
plt.xlabel("Lag")

plt.show()
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

stats.probplot(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm", plot=ax1)
ax1.set_title("QQ Plot (Log Returns) - German DAX")
ax1.axhline(y=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)
ax1.axvline(x=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)

stats.probplot(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm", plot=ax2)
ax2.set_title("QQ Plot (Log Returns) - US S&P500")
ax2.axhline(y=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)
ax2.axvline(x=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

stats.probplot(GDAXI["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm", plot=ax1)
ax1.set_title("QQ Plot (Absolute Returns) - German DAX")
ax1.axhline(y=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)
ax1.axvline(x=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)

stats.probplot(GSPC["Absolute Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm", plot=ax2)
ax2.set_title("QQ Plot (Absolute Returns) - US S&P500")
ax2.axhline(y=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)
ax2.axvline(x=0, linestyle='--', color='gray', alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

If we exemine the stylized facts about univariate financial returns:

* (U1) : Returns series are not iid although they show little serial correlation * (U2) : Series of absolute or squared returns show profound serial correlation * (U3) : Conditional expected returns are close to zero * (U4) : Volatility (conditional standard deviation) appears to vary over time * (U5) : Extreme returns appear in clusters * (U6) : Return series leptokurtic or heavy-tailed (power-like tail)

German DAX
US S&P 500

If we exemine the stylized facts about multivariate financial returns:

* (M1) : Multivariate return series show little evidence of cross-correlation, expect for contemporaneous returns (i.e. at the same time t) * (M2) : Multivariate series of absolute or squared returns show profound cross-correlation * (M3) : Correlations between contemporaneous returns vary over time * (M4) : Extreme returns in one series often coincide with extreme returns in several other series (i.e. tail dependence)

* (M1) We cannot assess this with the above * (M2) We cannot assess this with the above * (M3) We cannot assess this with the above * (M4) Yes, indeed this seems to be the case as discussed before

GDAXI_lr_mu = np.mean(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"])
GDAXI_lr_sigma = np.std(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"],ddof=1)

GSPC_lr_mu = np.mean(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"])
GSPC_lr_sigma = np.std(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"],ddof=1)
print("GDAXI ----")
print(f"mu={GDAXI_lr_mu}, sigma={GDAXI_lr_sigma}\n")
print("GSPC ----")
print(f"mu={GSPC_lr_mu}, sigma={GSPC_lr_sigma}")
GDAXI ----
mu=0.00013306635502387712, sigma=0.014908239478841886

GSPC ----
mu=0.0001794844039518181, sigma=0.012552419077231765
f
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))

plt.subplot(1,2,1)
res1 = stats.probplot(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm")
res2 = stats.probplot(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist=stats.t(df=4))
plt.scatter(res1[0][0], res1[0][1], s=5, color='blue', alpha=0.5, label="Normal")
plt.scatter(res2[0][0], res2[0][1], s=5, color='red', alpha=0.5, label="t (df=4)")
plt.plot(res1[0][0], res1[1][1] + res1[1][0]*res1[0][0], 'b--', alpha=0.3)
plt.plot(res2[0][0], res2[1][1] + res2[1][0]*res2[0][0], 'r--', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("QQ Plot - German DAX")
plt.legend()

plt.subplot(1,2,2)
res1 = stats.probplot(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist="norm")
res2 = stats.probplot(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna(), dist=stats.t(df=4))
plt.scatter(res1[0][0], res1[0][1], s=5, color='blue', alpha=0.5, label="Normal")
plt.scatter(res2[0][0], res2[0][1], s=5, color='red', alpha=0.5, label="t (df=4)")
plt.plot(res1[0][0], res1[1][1] + res1[1][0]*res1[0][0], 'b--', alpha=0.3)
plt.plot(res2[0][0], res2[1][1] + res2[1][0]*res2[0][0], 'r--', alpha=0.3)
plt.title("QQ Plot - US S&P500")
plt.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
from scipy.stats import norm

data = np.sort(GDAXI["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
n = len(data)
empirical_cdf = np.arange(1, n+1) / n

data2 = np.sort(GSPC["Log Return (Closing)"].dropna())
n2 = len(data2)
empirical_cdf2 = np.arange(1, n2+1) / n2

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))

plt.subplot(1,2,1)
plt.scatter(norm.cdf(data, loc=GDAXI_lr_mu, scale=GDAXI_lr_sigma), empirical_cdf, s=2, color='blue', label="Normal")
plt.scatter(stats.t.cdf(data, df=4, loc=GDAXI_lr_mu, scale=GDAXI_lr_sigma), empirical_cdf, s=2, color='red', label="t (df=4)")
plt.plot([0,1], [0,1], 'k--', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlabel("Theoretical CDF")
plt.ylabel("Empirical CDF")
plt.title("P-P Plot - German DAX")
plt.legend()

plt.subplot(1,2,2)
plt.scatter(norm.cdf(data2, loc=GSPC_lr_mu, scale=GSPC_lr_sigma), empirical_cdf2, s=2, color='blue', label="Normal")
plt.scatter(stats.t.cdf(data2, df=4, loc=GSPC_lr_mu, scale=GSPC_lr_sigma), empirical_cdf2, s=2, color='red', label="t (df=4)")
plt.plot([0,1], [0,1], 'k--', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlabel("Theoretical CDF")
plt.ylabel("Empirical CDF")
plt.title("P-P Plot - US S&P500")
plt.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
x = np.linspace(-0.15, 0.15, 1000)

q05_GDAXI = np.quantile(data, 0.05)
q95_GDAXI = np.quantile(data, 0.95)
q05_GSPC = np.quantile(data2, 0.05)
q95_GSPC = np.quantile(data2, 0.95)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 14))

# Full view
plt.subplot(3,2,1)
plt.hist(data, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GDAXI_lr_mu, GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GDAXI_lr_mu, scale=GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.xlim(-0.05, 0.05)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("PDF - German DAX")
plt.legend()

plt.subplot(3,2,2)
plt.hist(data2, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GSPC_lr_mu, GSPC_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GSPC_lr_mu, scale=GSPC_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.xlim(-0.05, 0.05)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("PDF - US S&P500")
plt.legend()

# Left tail
plt.subplot(3,2,3)
plt.hist(data, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GDAXI_lr_mu, GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GDAXI_lr_mu, scale=GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.axvline(x=q05_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='green', alpha=0.7, label=f"5% = {q05_GDAXI:.4f}")
plt.xlim(data.min(), q05_GDAXI)
plt.ylim(0, 3)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("Left Tail - German DAX")
plt.legend(fontsize=7)

plt.subplot(3,2,4)
plt.hist(data2, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GSPC_lr_mu, GSPC_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GSPC_lr_mu, scale=GSPC_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.axvline(x=q05_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='green', alpha=0.7, label=f"5% = {q05_GSPC:.4f}")
plt.xlim(data2.min(), q05_GSPC)
plt.ylim(0, 3)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("Left Tail - US S&P500")
plt.legend(fontsize=7)

# Right tail
plt.subplot(3,2,5)
plt.hist(data, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GDAXI_lr_mu, GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GDAXI_lr_mu, scale=GDAXI_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.axvline(x=q95_GDAXI, linestyle='--', color='orange', alpha=0.7, label=f"95% = {q95_GDAXI:.4f}")
plt.xlim(q95_GDAXI, data.max())
plt.ylim(0, 3)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("Right Tail - German DAX")
plt.legend(fontsize=7)

plt.subplot(3,2,6)
plt.hist(data2, bins=200, density=True, alpha=0.3, color='gray', label="Empirical")
plt.plot(x, norm.pdf(x, GSPC_lr_mu, GSPC_lr_sigma), 'b-', label="Normal")
plt.plot(x, stats.t.pdf(x, df=4, loc=GSPC_lr_mu, scale=GSPC_lr_sigma), 'r-', label="t (df=4)")
plt.axvline(x=q95_GSPC, linestyle='--', color='orange', alpha=0.7, label=f"95% = {q95_GSPC:.4f}")
plt.xlim(q95_GSPC, data2.max())
plt.ylim(0, 3)
plt.xlabel("Log Return")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.title("Right Tail - US S&P500")
plt.legend(fontsize=7)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
merged = pd.merge(GDAXI, GSPC, on="Date", how="outer", suffixes=("_GDAXI", "_GSPC"), indicator=True)

plt.plot(merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"],'.')
plt.xlabel("Log returns German DAX")
plt.ylabel("Log returns US S&P 500")
mask = merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"].notna() & merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"].notna()
res = stats.linregress(
    merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],
    merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"]
)
y_hat = (res.intercept+res.slope*merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"])
plt.plot(merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],y_hat,label=f"fit, $R^2$ = {round(res.rvalue,2)}")
plt.legend()
plt.show()


res2 = stats.linregress(
    merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"],
    merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"]
    
)
y_hat2 = (res2.intercept+res2.slope*merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"])

plt.plot(merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"],merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],'.')
plt.plot(merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"],y_hat2,label=f"fit, $R^2$ = {round(res2.rvalue,2)}")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

np.corrcoef(merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"])
mask_left_5 = mask & (
    (merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"] <= merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"].quantile(0.05)) &
    (merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"] <= merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"].quantile(0.05))
)

mask_right_5 = mask & (
    (merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"] >= merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"].quantile(0.95)) &
    (merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"] >= merged["Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"].quantile(0.95))
)
# Left tail regression
res_left = stats.linregress(
    merged.loc[mask_left_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],
    merged.loc[mask_left_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"]
)
x_left = merged.loc[mask_left_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"]

plt.plot(x_left, merged.loc[mask_left_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"], '.')
plt.plot(x_left.sort_values(), res_left.intercept + res_left.slope * x_left.sort_values(), 'r-')
plt.title(f"Left 5% — slope={res_left.slope:.4f}, r²={res_left.rvalue**2:.4f}")
plt.show()

# Right tail regression
res_right = stats.linregress(
    merged.loc[mask_right_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],
    merged.loc[mask_right_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"]
)
x_right = merged.loc[mask_right_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"]

plt.plot(x_right, merged.loc[mask_right_5, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"], '.')
plt.plot(x_right.sort_values(), res_right.intercept + res_right.slope * x_right.sort_values(), 'r-')
plt.title(f"Right 5% — slope={res_right.slope:.4f}, r²={res_right.rvalue**2:.4f}")
plt.show()

The stylized facts of multivariate time series * (M1) : The above shows that indeed cross-correlation between the series at the same times that is at equivalent t's (but not at different lags) * (M2) : We cannot state anything about the cross-correlation of the absolute returns (unless we do further analysis) * (M3) : We cannot state anything about the variation of the contemporaneous correlation over time (unless we do further anlysis) * (M4) : Again the extreme seem to happen around the same time (not explicitly though as shown above)

plt.plot(merged["Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"],y_hat,label=f"fit, $R^2$ = {round(res.rvalue,2)}")
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D

x = merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GDAXI"]
y = merged.loc[mask, "Log Return (Closing)_GSPC"]

# Zoom: clip to 1st–99th percentile
xlim = (x.quantile(0.01), x.quantile(0.99))
ylim = (y.quantile(0.01), y.quantile(0.99))
xz = x[(x >= xlim[0]) & (x <= xlim[1]) & (y >= ylim[0]) & (y <= ylim[1])]
yz = y[(x >= xlim[0]) & (x <= xlim[1]) & (y >= ylim[0]) & (y <= ylim[1])]

counts, xedges, yedges = np.histogram2d(xz, yz, bins=200)
Xc, Yc = np.meshgrid(xedges[:-1], yedges[:-1])

# a) Contour rings
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
ax.contour(Xc, Yc, counts.T, levels=12, cmap='plasma')
ax.set_xlabel("GDAXI"); ax.set_ylabel("GSPC")
ax.set_title("Contour Rings")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# b) Heatmap
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
h = ax.hist2d(xz, yz, bins=200, cmap='inferno')
plt.colorbar(h[3], ax=ax, label='Count')
ax.set_xlabel("GDAXI"); ax.set_ylabel("GSPC")
ax.set_title("Heatmap")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# c) 3D surface
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.plot_surface(Xc, Yc, counts.T, cmap='inferno', edgecolor='none', alpha=0.9)
ax.set_xlabel("GDAXI")
ax.set_ylabel("GSPC")
ax.set_zlabel("Count")
ax.set_title("3D Histogram")
ax.view_init(elev=30, azim=135)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
